observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (i) reconcile the balances of the Cash Local Treasury account in their respective reports to reflect accurate and reliable balance appearing in the FS; and (ii) conduct a detailed review of the accounting and treasury records and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted;

b. Municipal Treasurer to promptly submit to the Audit Team copy of the Cashbooks for all Cash in Bank accounts; and

c. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the (i) BRS are properly supported with bank statements; (ii) bank statements attached to the BRS are stamped "received" by the MAO; and (iii) BRS are promptly submitted to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Supply Officer to (i) maintain Stock Cards for all inventories to account the receipt and issuance of supplies; (ii) conduct regular physical count of inventories; and (iii) ensure the timely preparation and submission of the RPCI to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; and

b. instruct the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate periodic reconciliation of their reports so that discrepancies, if any, are immediately detected and corrected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Property Custodian to (i) reconcile the RPCPPE with the Property Cards and ensure the timely submission of the report to the Office of the Auditor; (ii) report all the unserviceable properties in the IIRUP, properly supported with complete documentation for the subsequent disposal activities in accordance with COA rules and regulations; (iii) submit a satisfactory explanation for the failure to submit a request for inspection from COA prior to the demolition of the Halfway House;

b. Property Custodian and Municipal Accountant to conduct a joint reconciliation of their respective reports and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and

c. Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the Notes to FS the demolished PPE valued at ₱99,223.20.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the BAC and its Secretariat and TWG to (i) submit written justification/explanation on the noted lapses and deficiencies; (ii) submit all noted lacking bid documents to the Audit Team for evaluation; (iii) furnish the Audit Team with copy of the contracts and its attachments within five calendar days from issuance thereof as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking documents for verification; and (ii) check the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and (c) require the Municipal Engineer to (i) provide valid explanation on the noted observations on the grant of time extension orders; and (ii) ensure that the pictures documenting the progress of the projects comply with DWPH D.O. No. 97.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the BAC to (i) submit written justification/explanation on the noted lapses and deficiencies; (ii) submit all lacking bid documents to the Audit Team for evaluation; (iii) furnish the Audit Team with copy of the contracts and its attachments within five calendar days from issuance thereof as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and

b. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) check the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and (ii) impose liquidated damages amounting to ₱3,130.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the concerned contractors to immediately correct and rectify the deficiencies noted on the above-mentioned projects to conform with the approved program of works, or in case of failure to satisfactorily rectify the noted defects, initiate the institution of appropriate legal actions against the erring contractors;

b. direct the Municipal Engineer to: (i) provide valid explanation on the noted observations on the grant of time extension orders; and (ii) submit immediately to the Audit Team the copies of Bar Chart, S-Curve, and Revised/Actual PERT/CPM, reflecting the planned/actual project accomplishments per month, to facilitate further evaluation of the COA-TAG and computation of the corresponding liquidated damages; and

c. direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance with the DPWH and procurement regulations in all future projects by addressing deficiencies in geotagging, project identification, and work specifications.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. instruct the HRMO to (i) thoroughly review all contracts before finalization to identify any errors and inconsistencies; (ii) continuously monitor contract validity to ensure compliance with terms; and (iii) strictly verify the DTRs, ARs and corresponding contracts before payroll preparation to ensure that all payments are properly supported and aligned with contractual terms;

b. instruct each department head to review the DTRs and ARs of job order personnel under their supervision prior to approval of the same to ensure that the DTRs are properly prepared and that the specific and actual work accomplishments are indicated in the individual ARs; and

c. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) check the veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (ii) coordinate with the HRMO regarding the overpayment of salaries totaling ₱23,000.00 made to JO personnel covered by a MOA, and cause the refund, if warranted.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

a. require the MDRRMO to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF by implementing the identified priority projects within the year through proper coordination with relevant offices to expedite fund utilization and implementation;

b. direct the MDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to (i) provide justification on the observations regarding the inconsistencies in the reporting of appropriations, utilizations and fund sources in the LDRRMF reports; and (ii) ensure regular reconciliation of the same to improve transparency, accuracy, and accountability in fund reporting; and

c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in accordance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the MPDC to ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1;

b. direct the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of development projects identified in the AIP to ensure that these projects are promptly and timely implemented in accordance with the schedule for the benefit of the constituents; and

c. to require the Municipal Budget Officer, with the review and approval of Sangguniang Bayan, to consider the reversion or re-appropriation of the savings from prior year’s completed project to other developmental projects for the optimal utilization of the said idle funds.

We reiterated our recommendations with some revisions that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all GAD-mandated PPAs are provided with adequate resources to warrant its implementation by sufficiently appropriating at least five percent of its annual budget to the GAD Fund.

We also recommended that the GAD Focal Person (i) revise the GPB should there be changes in the planned PPAs and properly inform the oversight bodies for any changes in the approved GPB; (ii) formulate PPAs that will enable the Municipality to collect gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data needed to update the GAD Database; (iii) prepare the GAD-related reports in accordance with the instructions provided in Annexes D and E of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01; and (iv) maximize the utilization of GAD fund, make sure that the identified priority projects will be implemented during the year, and closely monitor the implementation thereof to deliver satisfactory and timely benefits to the targeted beneficiaries.

We commended the efforts of the Municipality in complying with the aforementioned BIR regulation and encouraged continued timely withholding of taxes and remittance thereof. However, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to investigate the unreconciled balance of the Due to BIR account and effect adjusting entry/ies, if warranted.

Except on the noted periods where no remittances were made, we commended the Municipality on the prompt remittance of the contributions and loan amortizations due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth. However, we recommended that the MAO exert effort to investigate the unreconciled balances of the contributions/remittances to GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth and effect adjusting entry/ies, if warranted.

observations.recommendation:
We, therefore, recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to reclassify the semi-expendable properties of ₱230,478.80 by considering the PAJE embodied in the issued AOM and ensure henceforth, that all purchased PPEs shall be recorded into appropriate accounts following the asset recognition principle and the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00; and
b) Property Custodian to:
(i) exclude the above semi-expendable properties in the RPCPPE and ensure that all PPE items that passed the asset recognition principle and the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 shall be reported in the said report;
(ii) report all obsolete and unserviceable properties in the IIRUP duly signed by the Municipal Accountant, Inspection/Appraisal Committee and approved by the LCE, and submit the same to the Disposal Committee for proper disposition to avoid further deterioration;
(iii) keep all the unserviceable PPE in a secured place and dispose them within a reasonable time; and
(iii) ensure that the Audit Team will be informed of the upcoming conduct of physical count of inventories, property and equipment, and see to it that the corresponding reports are submitted on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant take necessary actions in settling the reconciling items amounting to ₱11,143.89 and collect from the bank the said amount should unauthorized debit against the Municipality’s account shall be warranted. Moreover, coordinate with all depository banks for the regular issuance of bank statements with copy of debit and credit memos and require the Municipal Accountant to promptly prepare and submit the BRS which shall be supported with bank statements to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the month end Cash in Bank balance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to forward the list of bank reconciling items together with a query to the COA Government Accountancy Sector (GAS) for appropriate treatment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Authorized Agency Officer (AAO) to timely prepare and submit the ARA to the GSIS each time changes are effected in the payroll, for the GSIS to update its MSP and reflect the accurate amount of premiums and loan amortizations in the Billing Statement; and
b) Municipal Accountant (i) to reconcile the withholdings made in the payroll with the figures reflected in the Billing Statement, strictly monitor the postings made in the books of accounts, and ensure that the remittances are equal with the amount of withholdings for the month to prevent over/under remittances; and (ii) reconcile the outstanding balance of accumulated withheld premiums per books with the records of the GSIS, remit the unremitted premiums, and cause the refund to the respective employees.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to explain then provide to the Audit Team the noted documentary deficiencies in the procurement process; and remind the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to processing of vouchers for the payment to suppliers.
observations.managementAction: The Management, particularly the BAC, rechecked the transactions with noted deficiencies on the issued report and submitted on August 10, 2021 the collated lacking documents to the Audit Team. Moreover, for the lacking documents which could not be complied with, the management submitted their justifications to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: We have verified the submitted documents to the Audit Team, and we noted that most of the lacking documents were still not complied with such as the Request for Quotations (RFQs), Abstract of Quotations, Notice of Award (NOA), Mayor’s Permit, Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System (PhilGEPS) Registration Number, original copy of the Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS), Delivery Receipt, etc.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile thoroughly and present the correct amount of tax delinquencies in their respective records. Also, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies in intensifying the collection of real property tax such as information campaign to encourage taxpayers in taking advantage of early payment discount.
observations.managementAction: The Management, through the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, have already reconciled in CY 2021, and made necessary adjustments to effect the reconciliation through Journal Voucher Nos. 100-2021050652 and 200-202105046.
observations.reason: We have traced in Journal Voucher Nos. 100-2021050652 and 200-202105046 the adjustments made in the RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2021 totaling ₱21,681.84 and ₱27,102.31, respectively. However, we still noted that the year-end balance of RPT and SET Receivables did not tally with the List of all Real Property Tax Delinquencies. Still, no detailed information Campaign was conducted due to strict health protocols imposed within the Municipality thus, RPT and SET Collections of ₱524,441.36 in CY 2020 decreased to ₱400,845.92 in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to update the Audit Team of the status of the five PPAs and fast track their implementation in order to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management, disclosed that out of the three uncompleted projects in CY 2020, two of which were completed in the 1st of CY 2021 while the other one was completed in the 2nd quarter of the same year.
observations.reason: Verification of the Report on Government PPAs disclosed that out of the five uncompleted PPAs in CY 2020 under 20 percent Development Fund, two of which were realigned and one was completed in the 1st quarter of CY 2021. While the remaining two projects were also completed in the 2nd quarter of CY 2021. However, we still observed the same findings in CY 2021 audit thus, we reiterated the audit findings and recommendations in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: enjoin the LDRRMO to make sure that the continuing appropriations will be implemented in the following year; and instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of CY 2014 LDRRMF to the General Fund which can be utilize for social services.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO ensures that the implementation of projects under continuing appropriations would reach by at least 80 percent in CY 2021, considering that the Municipality was still in pandemic. The amount of ₱80,132.19 representing unutilized funds for CY 2014 LDRRMF was already reverted to the General Fund with check number 0070646626 dated 5/3/2021.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an appropriate policy directive to cause the implementation of the five unimplemented PPAs and to direct the GFPS to observe timely submission of the GPB and its HGDG Checklist and the GAR and its HGDG PIMME Checklist including the other supporting reportorial contents; and allow the GFPS to attend capacity-building or training on how to administer GAD-related tools such as but not limited to the HGDG Checklist and GMEF Questionnaire.
observations.managementAction: The Management, particularly the GAD Focal Person, disclosed that appropriate policy for the implementation of five unimplemented projects in CY 2020 has yet to be done because the funds for such projects were already closed as at year-end.
observations.reason: No policy was directed to implement the four uncompleted PPAs in CY 2021. However, the project Construction of Agdamagan Footbridge was funded and started in CY 2021, and completed in the 1st quarter of CY 2022. The GAD Focal Person disclosed that GPB for CY 2021, GAR for CY 2020, and its related HGDG Checklist were all submitted on March 26, 2021. We have verified the reports submitted by the GFPS and we noted that the GPB and GAR for CY 2021 were only submitted on March 21, 2022 and January 24, 2022, respectively, while the related HGDG Checklist were not submitted at all. Also observed GAR for CY2021 was still not supported with the required reportorial contents. We reiterated the audit findings and recommendations in Part II of this report. Review of the submitted reports related to GAD in CY 2021, confirmed that the LGU has yet to accomplish the GMEF Organizational Assessment Questionnaire thus, assessment of the LGU’s GAD mainstreaming work could still not be attained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer follow-up the adjustments that have to be made by the PNB Romblon Branch concerning the bank reconciling items of ₱11,143.89. Moreover, coordinate with the depository banks for their prompt issuance of bank statements and require the Municipal Accountant to submit the BRS on time, duly supported with official bank statements.
observations.managementAction: Management sent a letter to the depositary bank on March 3, 2020, requesting the latter to draw an adjustment in view of the noted unadjusted bank reconciling items. Management also said that they will exert more effort in obtaining bank statements and in the preparation and submission of BRS to the audit team.
observations.reason: The noted bank reconciling items still remained uncorrected as at December 31, 2021. Moreover, we still noted 52 BRS which were submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2021, and 25 BRS were prepared with no corresponding bank statements. Thus, we reiterated the audit findings and recommendations in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the AAO to prepare and submit ARA to the GSIS for the latter to update its MSP and Billing Statement; and require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, who prepares the payroll, to reconcile the withholdings with the figures reflected in the Billing Statement.
observations.managementAction: Management, through the Accounting Office, partially reconciled the accounts with the GSIS and remitted an amount of ₱667,725.53.
observations.reason: Still, we noted unreconciled balances of the withholdings and remittances to the GSIS as at December 31, 2021 thus, resulted in over remittance of ₱70,771.66 this time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PEER to upload diligently the necessary reports on the EPRS for the system to update its SOA and PEPRL; and require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, who prepares the payroll, to reconcile the withholdings with the figures reflected in the PEPRL.
observations.managementAction: Management through the Accounting Office, partially reconciled the accounts with the PHIC and remitted an amount of ₱11,078.77 on June 11, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: the Municipal Engineer to submit the approved Plans/Drawings and Specifications; the BAC explain the noted deficiencies and submit all lacking bid documents to the Audit Team and henceforth adhere strictly to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in all stages of procurement process and ensure that all bid documents are complete, evaluated and submitted on time; and, the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims for payment of the above infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted to the Audit Team their justifications and the noted lacking documents on June 23, 2020.
observations.reason: Still, no proof of posting in the PhilGEPS website was submitted for the contracts and NTP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: the contractor to repair the items found to be defective, install the items missing and provide the lacking documents which were noted to be necessary in computing the contract time and amount; the Municipal Engineer to determine the need to apply the warranty security should the contractor refuse to take remedial actions on the above projects; and to monitor and assess the contractor’s compliance with the recommendation and apprise the same to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management sent a letter dated June 23, 2020 to the contractor for the payment of liquidated damages. Then, the latter authorized the former to deduct liquidated damages from retention money. On September 28, 2020, management submitted a Disbursement Voucher showing the release of retention money, net of liquidated damages of ₱12,114.06.
observations.reason: Pertinent copies of PAG-ASA weather reports and an approved Quality Control Program (QCP) were still not submitted to the Audit Team. Moreover, we have not received reports/ and or proof from the Engineering Office that the contractor had repaired the items found to be defective and with missing items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, who also heads the Municipal Solid Waste Board, establish a Sanitary Landfill in compliance with Section 17(h) of Republic Act No. 9003; make sure that municipal MRF is properly monitored, maintained and operated for such purpose; and encourage active participation of the nine barangays to support the SWM program of the municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of wastes and the benefits to the environment of their participation in the implementation of SWM program.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO has already coordinated with the Office of the Municipal Engineer the preparation or crafting of Sanitary Landfill design. Management also rented heavy equipment for the site clearing and for the closure of MRF.
observations.reason: Still, the Municipality has not yet started the construction of their Sanitary Landfill. The Audit Team continuously monitors the solid waste management program of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor a) require the BAC to explain then provide to the Audit Team the noted documentary deficiencies in the procurement process; and b) remind the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to processing of vouchers for the payment to suppliers.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile thoroughly and to present the correct amount of tax delinquencies in their respective records. Also, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies in intensifying the collection of real property tax such as information campaign to encourage taxpayers in taking advantage of early payment discount.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to update the Audit Team of the status of the five PPAs and fast track their implementation in order to maximize the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) enjoin the LDRRMO to make sure that the continuing appropriations will be implemented in the following year; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of CY 2014 LDRRMF to the General Fund which can be utilize for social services.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
issue an appropriate policy directive to cause the implementation of the five unimplemented PPAs and to direct the GFPS to monitor them;
enjoin the GFPS to observe timely submission of the GPB and its HGDG Checklist and the GAR and its HGDG PIMME Checklist including the other supporting reportorial contents; and
allow the GFPS to attend capacity-building or training on how to administer or accomplish the GAD-related tools such as but not limited to the HGDG Checklist and GMEF Questionnaire.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to comply with DOF Department Order No. 27-05 and Department Circular No. 001-2015 relative to a sound and efficient management of government funds by transferring all accounts from private bank to AGDB.
observations.managementAction: They are now in the process of applying internet banking facility with the DBP.
observations.reason: The Management informed the Audit Team that they could not yet close the bank account with the PNB because it is still being used for online remittances to BIR.

observations.recommendation: With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer follow-up the adjustments that have to be made by the PNB Romblon Branch concerning the bank reconciling items of P11,143.89. Moreover, coordinate with the depository banks for their prompt issuance of bank statements and require the Municipal Accountant to submit the BRS on time, duly supported with official bank statements.

With that, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt strategies in intensifying the collection of real property tax such as information campaign to encourage taxpayers to take advantage of early payment discount; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their records and present the correct amount of tax delinquencies; and (c) request the assistance of the Office of the Provincial Assessor in the conduct of tax mapping and advertise by posting at conspicuous places the intent of the municipality to hire a licensed person for the position of Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant to fully reconcile the results of the physical count to the balances in accounting records; and (b) ensure that the Property Custodian submits on time the RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I.
The Management promised to do reconciliation.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the withholding and remittances of taxes by diligently preparing the subsidiary and general ledgers that must tally with the actual withholdings and remittances made to the BIR to avoid the condition of under remittances.
The Municipal Accountant in her letter dated April 15, 2020 commented that their SL tallies with the GL as it was prepared based on the understanding of the employee in-charge of maintaining the record.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
Recomputation of the Due to BIR account showed that the withholdings and remittances in the SL do not tally with the posting made in the GL.

With that, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the AAO to prepare and submit ARA to the GSIS for the latter to update its MSP and Billing Statement; and (b) require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, who prepares the payroll, to reconcile the withholdings with the figures reflected in the Billing Statement.
The Management in the same letter commented that they already reconciled their accounts with the GSIS and remitted a total amount of P663,792.17 for the months of January and February 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

the Municipal Engineer to submit the approved Plans/Drawings and   Specifications;
the BAC explain the noted deficiencies and submit all lacking bid documents to the Audit Team and henceforth adhere strictly to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in all stages of procurement process and ensure that all bid documents are complete, evaluated and submitted on time; and
the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims for payment of the above infrastructure projects.
The Management explained in a letter dated February 21, 2020 that the BAC Secretariat overlooked the copies of approved Plans/Drawings/ Specifications, PERT/CPM, Bill of Quantities, Abstract of Bid as Calculated, Notice to Proceed and evidence of PhilGEPS posting while collating the documents. They promised to submit them to the Audit Team.
The Management also explained that the Construction Method, List of Key Personnel, Pledged Equipment, Organizational Structure, NFCC and Omnibus Sworn Statement were just overlooked during bid evaluation. They also promised to submit them to the Audit Team.
For the Tax Clearance, however, they asserted that the document submitted was not expired.
Auditor’s Rejoinder
The Tax Clearance attached to the contract was issued by the BIR on December 14, 2017 and has a validity of one year from issuance.  Hence, it already expired on December 14, 2018. However, it was still used in the bid opening of the two projects on January 28, 2019.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with Item 8 of Annex E of the same revised IRR for the imposition of liquidated damages against the defaulting contractor.
The Management commented in a letter dated March 11, 2020 that the imposition of liquidated damages was not made because the Municipal Accountant relied on the STE submitted by the contractor which shows no incurrence of delay. However, to comply with the audit observation and recommendation, the Management sent demand letter to the contractor for the payment of liquidated damages.

With that, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require (a) the contractor to repair the items found to be defective, install the items discovered to be missing and provide the lacking documents which were noted to be necessary in computing the contract time and amount; (b) the Municipal Engineer to determine the need to apply the warranty security should the contractor refuse to take remedial actions on the above projects; and (c) the Municipal Accountant ensure the proper imposition of liquidated damages for the delayed completion of the projects.
Also, require the Municipal Engineer to determine the need to apply the retention money (defect liability period) and the warranty security (structural defects and failures) should the contractor refuse to take remedial actions on the above projects. He shall likewise monitor and assess the contractor’s compliance with the recommendation and apprise the same to the Audit Team.
The Management explained in a letter dated January 24, 2020 that for project nos. 1, 2 and 3, the contractor promised to repair and submit the lacking reports within the first quarter of year 2020. They also promised to furnish the Audit Team of pictures upon completion. The Management also commented that the volume of concrete for project no. 4 was less than the required concrete for material testing and the material test result for project no. 5 will be submitted.
For project no. 6, Management commented that the broken glass, faucet and shower at Barangay Agutay were not included in the project and they said that the contractor will start installing the ceiling as soon as delivery of roofing materials is completed. As to the project at Barangays Ipil and Jao-Asan, the broken glass was not likewise included in the project. The contractor however promised to install the faucet and tiles as soon as possible.
For project no.7, Management commented that they forwarded a copy of TAS report to the contractor. They also explained that the approved plan of the project came from the National Meat Inspection Service (NMIS) Office and that upon project completion and NMIS site inspection, the only recommendation made was the removal of the stair at loading bay.
For project no. 8, Management admitted that they overlooked the imposition of liquidated damages for the delay incurred in the completion of project. They explained that it was unintentional and a mere result of reliance on the verified STE. Upon rechecking of files, they said to have found copies of approved request for time extension, suspension orders and order to resume which might have not been forwarded to TAS. They recomputed the time elapsed and reached to a conclusion that the contractor has indeed incurred 29 days of delay. Consequently, a letter was sent to the proprietor of Mt. Guiting-Guiting Construction, requesting him to refund the equivalent amount of liquidated damages.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, who also heads the Municipal Solid Waste Board, (a) establish a Sanitary Landfill in compliance with Section 17(h) of Republic Act No. 9003; (b) make sure that municipal MRF is properly monitored, maintained and operated for such purpose so that it will not further become an uncontrolled open dumpsite; and (c) encourage active participation of the nine barangays to support the SWM program of the municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of wastes and the benefits to the environment of their participation in the implementation of SWM program.

With that, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the LDRRMO to justify or explain the non-implementation of the PPAs, low fund utilization rate and the transfer of the unutilized/unexpended            MF-Capital Outlay to special trust fund. Henceforth, ensure that the PPAs identified for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery are implemented and the funds therefor are utilized for the benefits of the constituents.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an appropriate policy directive to implement the PPAs provided for in the GPB and that directive shall mandate the GFPS to monitor its implementation in compliance with Item 4.1.C of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. Moreover, the GFPS shall make an effort to increase the utilization rate of the PPAs being charged to the GAD Fund in compliance with the same JMC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to submit all monthly and quarterly financial and accounting reports and their corresponding statements within the prescribed period in order for the Audit Team to undertake timely review and post-audit of the transactions and accounts and at the same time to inform management of any defects or deficiencies.
The Management admitted that they sometimes commit delays on the submission of accounts and reports due to bad weather condition, unavailability of personnel, late issuance of checks and other unforeseen circumstances. they promised to exert more effort for them to be able to submit on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that: (a) the Municipal Accountant secure evidence to prove the earned interest income; (b) the Municipal Treasurer secure the latest bank statement and update her Cashbook; (c) the Municipal Treasurer immediately inform the bank regarding its reconciling items; and (d) the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant inform the payee of the stale check and perform the necessary process to verify, cancel and replace the above stale checks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the interest income; The Municipal Treasurer updated her cashbook; Copies of letters dated June 9, 2014, July 23, 2014, November 4, 2014, August 6, 2015 were furnished to the Audit Team to prove that they have informed the bank about the bank reconciling items; Stale check of P50.00 was adjusted to payable while another one worth P547.20 was cancelled and replaced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Municipal Accountant monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advance per accountable officer in compliance with Item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and send demand letter to those who failed to liquidate after the lapse of deadline, copy furnish the Audit Team in compliance with Item V.2 of COA Memorandum No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Management apprised the Audit Team of their “no liquidation of cash advance, no salary”. Also, they have sent demand letters to accountable officers who failed to liquidate outstanding cash advances as proved by the demand letters dated June 27, 2018 and August 8, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the existing discrepancy between the balances per books and RPCI; and (b) to substantiate the sale of inventories totaling P17,048.42 and require the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to reflect in the books the correct inventory balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the amount of P17,048.42 pertaining to merchandise inventory as this, according to the MSWDO, was already consumed/distributed as relief goods during Typhoon Nona.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer return the unexpended fund totalling P39,890.41 to its respective source agency and secure Official Receipt for the same in compliance with Item Nos. 4.9 and 5.5 of COA Circular No. 94-013; (b) the Municipal Accountant reclassify the amount of P3,132.04 to reflect its income nature in compliance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer returned the fund balances to the NGAs as follows: The amount of P39,800.90 per Check No. 305666; The amount of P89.51 per Check No. 305662; and The amount of P3,132.04 per Check No. 442171.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget is submitted on time and endorsed by the DILG in compliance with Item 4.3 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01; (b) implement the GAD-related PPAs of the municipality in compliance with Item 4.1.C.1.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (c) provide to the Audit Team the HGDG PIMME of all attributed PPAs to the GAD Accomplishment Report in compliance with Item 7.3 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: The Management apprised the Audit Team that GAD-related PPAs were not implemented due to non-endorsement of the DILG. They, however, implemented some PPAs using general fund as not to deprive their constituents of the benefits of PPAs. Hence, GAD Fund for CY 2018 is still unexpended. As to the endorsement of the DILG, they were able to secure Certificate of Review and Endorsement for the GPB for CY 2019.
observations.reason: According to GAD Focal Person on June 5, 2020, the 2018 GAD Fund was still not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, who also heads the Municipal Solid Waste Board, to; (a) establish a Sanitary Landfill to comply with Item (h) of Section 17 R.A. No. 9003; and (b) encourage active participation of the 9 barangays to support the SWM program of the municipality by educating their respective constituents through Information Education Campaign on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the importance of their participation in the implementation of SWM programs.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO is now in the process of updating the SWM to include the sanitary landfill. They also informed the public on the proper waste segregation thru assembly meetings, caravans and symposiums.
observations.reason: The municipality is only in the process of integrating category 1 sanitary landfill to their SWM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to fast track the implementation of the unexpended/unobligated Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management furnished the Audit Team of pictures of the ongoing construction of the DRRM Multi-Purpose Center.
observations.reason: No information was provided to the Audit Team on the purchase of furniture and fixtures for the Evacuation Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to submit all lacking bid documents to the Audit Team and henceforth adhere strictly to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in all stages of procurement process and ensure that all bid documents are complete, evaluated and submitted on time.
observations.managementAction: The Management furnished the Audit Team most of the lacking bid documents. They also said that late submission of POs and Contracts was due to lack of full time BAC Secretariat member. Also, non-posting of NOA, NTP and Contract to the PhilGEPS was due to weak internet connection in their area.
observations.reason: Submitted most of the lacking bid documents to the Audit Team. However, with regards to posting of NOA, NTP and contracts to PhilGEPS, only certification was submitted that they could not post the said documents due to poor internet connection. Hence, posting must still be complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims for payment of the above infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided some of the noted lacking documents such as Warranty Security, Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC), Sales Invoice and Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR).
observations.reason: Our evaluation of submitted documents disclosed that: No Warranty Security was still provided for Water Testing Kits, Multi-Purpose Shredder and two units of Harvester; No Clearance from Provincial Treasurer that the gravel and sand fees have been paid was still provided; and No evidence of posting of NOA and Contract to the PhilGEPS was provided which they said was due to weak internet signal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) fast track the implementation of identified developmental PPAs and maximize the utilization of 20% Development Fund; and (b) provide the Audit Team copies of SB Resolution approving the identified individual PAPs funded out of lump sum/generic appropriation and henceforth, make sure that all PPAs covered by a lump sum appropriation be supported with SB Resolution in compliance with Item 2 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 96-263 dated December 9, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided the Audit Team of Appropriation Ordinance No. 3 series of 2019 which realigned the projects Purchase of Land (Partial Payment) for the Establishment of Manpower/Training Center (Tampayan) worth P4,000,000.00 and Road opening of Binaliwan-Beatriz FMR worth P1,000,000.00 deemed to other developmental projects approved by the MDC Resolution No. 2018-14 and MDC Resolution No. 2018-10.
observations.reason: No information was provided to the Audit Team on the status of new PPAs (after realignment); and No information was provided on the status of lump sum appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: (a) require close monitoring of the project Dulangan Spillway; (b) secure latest result for Water Sample Test/Potability Test and submit the approved As-Built Plans for the project Expansion of Potable Water System Supply Level II (Phase III); (c) require the installation of items found to be non-existent in the projects Road Opening of Pondoy-Lumambao FMR Phase I; and (d) coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the latter’s imposition of liquidated damages in compliance with Section 68 of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided the Audit Team copy of Water Sampling Result by the Rural Health Unit with recommendation that regular cleaning of water source/tank chlorination at source be done.
observations.reason: RCPC 610m and stone masonry for STA 0+500 is still for construction as certified by the Municipal Engineer. As to Dulangan Spillway, no update/information was received at our end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous recommendation that the BAC, or other officer assigned, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from the execution and purchase orders within five (5) working days from issuance, pursuant to COA Circular no. 2009-001 dated February 12, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same and enable us promptly inform the management of the defects or deficiencies which may be noted therefrom.
observations.managementAction: BAC secretariat persistently and continuously strived to follow the regulation despite the existence of the cited reasons for delays which are beyond their controls.
observations.reason: Due to geographical constraints, lack of personnel, some factors such as travel outside the town were the causes of delays. But the major issue is the multi-function of every member of BAC and BAC Secretariat. They have other activities to attend to, being demanded by other equally important responsibilities, including but not limited to the duties of their primary function.

observations.recommendation: Recommended that the Municipal Mayor: Ensure compliance to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The municipality adhered to the Joint Memo Circular mentioned in the Audit Recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: Ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund and full implementation of the projects funded out of the fund for the benefit of its constituents; and Development Projects Ensure strict adherence to the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of the project was made. Remaining funds are intended for payment of 100% accomplishments.
observations.reason: Last year’s unimplemented projects were already started/implemented but there were still unimplemented projects in the current year.
